guide_html:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT IELTS TASK 1

1. Dạng bài

  • Mixed charts: Bar chart \+ Pie chart
  • Nội dung: living alone (age \+ gender) \+ housing size

2. Introduction (công thức)

  • bar chart \+ pie chart \+ living alone \+ bedrooms \+ England \+ 2011

→ dùng: illustrate / compare / show

3. Overview (ý chính, không số)

  • Nhóm trẻ: nam cao hơn nữ
  • Nhóm lớn tuổi: nữ cao hơn nam
  • Trung niên: gần như cân bằng
  • Nhà nhỏ (1–2 phòng): chiếm đa số
  • Nhà lớn (4+ phòng): chiếm tỷ lệ rất nhỏ

4. Body 1 – Bar chart (Age \+ Gender)

Chia theo nhóm tuổi:

16–34

  • nam \> nữ

35–64

  • gần bằng nhau
  • có điểm cân bằng

65+

  • nữ \> nam
  • khoảng cách tăng theo tuổi

→ Key idea:

  • xu hướng đảo chiều theo độ tuổi

5. Body 2 – Pie chart (Bedrooms)

Theo thứ tự:

  • 2 bedrooms: lớn nhất
  • 1 và 3 bedrooms: chiếm phần lớn
  • 4 bedrooms: nhỏ
  • 5+ bedrooms: nhỏ nhất

→ Key idea:

  • nhà ít phòng chiếm ưu thế

6. Từ vựng cần dùng

  • account for
  • the majority / the minority
  • outnumber
  • be evenly distributed
  • dominate
  • make up a small proportion

The charts illustrate the proportions of males and females living alone in England in 2011 across different age groups, along with the distribution of bedroom numbers in one-person households.

Overall, younger age groups were dominated by men living alone, whereas women accounted for the majority in older age brackets. In addition, most one-person households occupied properties with either two or three bedrooms.

Among younger individuals, men were more likely to live alone. Specifically, males represented 53% of those aged 16–24 and 63% in the 25–34 group, with a peak of 65% among people aged 35–49. The gender gap then narrowed in the 50–64 group, where both sexes accounted for an equal share of 50%. Beyond this age, the pattern reversed, with women becoming increasingly dominant. Females comprised 63% of those aged 65–74, rising to 72% for the 75–84 group and peaking at 75% among individuals aged 85 and over. Overall, women made up a larger proportion of solo dwellers (56%) compared to men (44%).

Regarding housing size, two-bedroom homes were the most common among single occupants, accounting for 35.4% of cases. This was followed by three-bedroom properties at 29.8% and one-bedroom dwellings at 28.0%. Larger homes were relatively rare, with only 5.3% of individuals living in four-bedroom properties and a minimal 1.4% occupying homes with five or more bedrooms.
